frameworks.name: structured multi-armed bandits
frameworks.one_line: bandits whose options share a latent functional structure across positions

frameworks.name: function learning
frameworks.one_line: learning the latent reward function that ties options together

frameworks.name: clustering of reward distributions
frameworks.one_line: grouping rounds by similar reward distributions to reuse knowledge

frameworks.name: uncertainty-guided exploration
frameworks.one_line: exploration directed by posterior uncertainty over options

core_question: How do humans use latent structure across options to guide exploration in multi-armed bandits?
key_assumptions: options can share an underlying latent function (e.g., linear in spatial position)
humans can generalize learned structure across rounds
exploration and exploitation must be traded off
manipulations: presence vs. absence of latent functional structure across options
key_findings: participants use functional structure to guide exploration
learning-to-learn effect: faster identification of the latent function across rounds
results replicate with non-linear functions and rule out heuristic accounts
interpretations: best-fitting model combines function learning, clustering across rounds, and uncertainty-guided exploration
human reinforcement learning exploits latent structure to improve efficiency
